Container.Name: n8n
Container.Overview:
n8n is a powerful workflow-automation tool: connect 400+ apps and APIs, build flows visually, and drop into code whenever you need to. This is a plug-and-play Unraid Community Applications template that wraps the official n8nio/n8n image, tuned with sensible production defaults.
IMPORTANT - two one-time setup steps (see the README): 1. DATABASE: this template defaults to PostgreSQL (recommended). Create a database and user first, then fill in the Postgres Host / Database / User / Password fields below. (To use SQLite instead, set DB Type to 'sqlite' and ignore the Postgres fields.) 2. PERMISSIONS: n8n runs as user 'node' (UID 1000) and has no PUID/PGID. The AppData folder must be writable by UID 1000 - run once on the Unraid console: chown -R 1000:1000 /mnt/user/appdata/n8n
Included defaults: task runners on, execution-history pruning (no DB bloat), binary data stored on disk, telemetry off, and N8N_SECURE_COOKIE=false so login works over plain LAN HTTP (set it to true behind HTTPS).
Detailed README (full setup & options): https://github.com/junkerderprovinz/unraid-docker-templates/blob/main/n8n/README.md
Container.DonateText: If this template helps you, consider buying me a coffee.
Container.Requires: A reachable PostgreSQL server with a database and user created for n8n (template default). See the README for the SQL. To use SQLite instead, set DB Type to 'sqlite'. The AppData folder must be owned by UID 1000 (chown -R 1000:1000).

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: n8n web interface / editor port.

Config.Name: AppData (n8n data)
Config.Description: Persistent n8n data: encryption key, binary data, logs. MUST be owned by UID 1000 - run once: chown -R 1000:1000 /mnt/user/appdata/n8n

Config.Name: Local Files (/files)
Config.Description: Optional host folder the Read/Write Files node can access. Must also be writable by UID 1000.

Config.Name: DB Type
Config.Description: Database type. 'postgresdb' (recommended, template default) or 'sqlite'. For SQLite, set this to sqlite and leave the Postgres fields empty.

Config.Name: Postgres Host
Config.Description: Hostname or IP of your PostgreSQL server (e.g. the official PostgreSQL CA app, or 192.168.x.x). Required when DB Type is postgresdb.

Config.Name: Postgres Port
Config.Description: PostgreSQL port.

Config.Name: Postgres Database
Config.Description: Database name (create it first - see README).

Config.Name: Postgres User
Config.Description: Database user (create it first - see README).

Config.Name: Postgres Password
Config.Description: Password for the database user.

Config.Name: Encryption Key
Config.Description: Key used to encrypt stored credentials. STRONGLY recommended: set a long random value and back it up. If left empty, n8n generates one into the AppData folder (persist it, or saved credentials become unreadable).

Config.Name: Secure Cookie
Config.Description: Keep 'false' for LAN access over plain HTTP (otherwise the login is blocked). Set 'true' when running behind HTTPS.

Config.Name: Timezone (TZ)
Config.Description: Container timezone.

Config.Name: Generic Timezone
Config.Description: Timezone n8n uses for Schedule/Cron triggers. Keep equal to TZ.

Config.Name: Task Runners
Config.Description: Run Code-node tasks in a separate runner process (n8n recommended). 'true' or 'false'.

Config.Name: Prune Executions
Config.Description: Auto-delete old execution history so the database doesn't grow forever ('true'/'false').

Config.Name: Execution Max Age (hours)
Config.Description: How many hours of execution history to keep when pruning is on (336 = 14 days).

Config.Name: Binary Data Mode
Config.Description: Where to store binary data: 'filesystem' (recommended, on disk) or 'default' (in the database).

Config.Name: Telemetry
Config.Description: Anonymous usage diagnostics. 'false' disables telemetry.

Config.Name: Webhook URL
Config.Description: Public base URL for webhooks behind a reverse proxy, e.g. https://n8n.example.com/ . Leave empty for LAN-only use.

Config.Name: Host
Config.Description: Public hostname behind a reverse proxy, e.g. n8n.example.com . Leave empty for LAN-only use.

Config.Name: Protocol
Config.Description: 'http' for LAN. Usually keep 'http' and terminate TLS at your reverse proxy; use 'https' only if n8n itself serves TLS.
